arts.ttl: Unai Emery tips Ezri Konsa to follow in footsteps of Arsenal and Man City stars
arts.sum: Aston Villa and England defender Ezri Konsa has established himself as the club's starting right-back, having initially been reluctant to make the switch from central defence

arts.ttl: Ollie Watkins, Newcastle warning and Unai Emery's 'difficult summer' admission
arts.sum: "It depends, but last year we had very good experiences playing in the Conference League, but of course the Champions League is more difficult. Progressively he got better and, of course, to compete with Watkins is difficult but he had moments to play and his impact last year was sometimes like now he is doing.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa boss Unai Emery says Ollie Watkins ‘100 per cent’ fit to face Wolves
arts.sum: The England international came off in the second half of Villa’s debut Champions League win over Young Boys.

arts.ttl: Tyrone Mings and Boubacar Kamara injury updates as Unai Emery delivers 'good news'
arts.sum: Aston Villa manager Unai Emery has revealed Tyrone Mings and Boubacar Kamara are now in full training every day as they both close in on a return to action. Asked if he had been expecting Onana to chip in with goals so soon after joining, Emery responded: "He is important in everything.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa forward Jhon Duran's commitment "is getting better", says Unai Emery
arts.sum: Aston Villa head coach Unai Emery suggests that there are no guarantees that Jhon Duran will be rewarded with his first start of the season against Wolverhampton Wanderers.

arts.ttl: Premier League: Predictions for all eight games on Saturday
arts.sum: The Premier League continues this weekend and Tom Hill has highlighted our writers' best bets for all eight matches taking place on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Matchday Guide: Aston Villa v Wolves
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Wolverhampton Wanderers - Sat, Sep 21st 2024, 15:00 Aston Villa are back in B6 on Saturday to face Wolverhampton Wanderers in the Premier League. The Villans are aiming to follow up successive wins over Everton and BSC Young Boys in the past six days and extend our impressive start to the season. Here's everything you need to know ahead of the encounter to enjoy your visit to Villa Park on matchday... Getting to the game Click on the sections below to learn more about tickets, Villa Park and transport to the stadium. Mobile Tickets Villa Park is now fully NFC ticketing enabled for supporters to access the stadium. Near-Field Communication (NFC) enables fans to enter the stadium via Apple or Google Wallet. Click here for more information. Supporters can now also access their match-by-match tickets and enter the stadium directly via the Aston Villa app. Simply log into your Digital Account in the app and your tickets will appear under the ‘Tickets’ section. Supporters must update their app to the latest version to ensure in-app ticketing works without issue. Tickets A limited number of general admission tickets for the match remain. Book online here. Season ticket holders unable to attend can list their seat for resale online or by calling our ticket office on 0333 323 1874. Resale is open until 24 hours before kick-off. The best way to secure tickets for future matches at Villa Park is via a Membership. Get Matchday Ready Ahead of the match at Villa Park, the club would like to advise supporters of the following ticketing information to ensure everyone enjoys their visit to our stadium. Fans will need to provide one of the two specified ticket types to gain entry to the stadium - access tickets in our official Aston Villa mobile app or download to Apple/Google Wallet for NFC entry. More information can be found here. Hospitality and Premium Experiences The club has a range of new hospitality and GA+ areas to complement our existing offer. Check out all the information here and book online. Hospitality bookings for our upcoming home matches can be made over the phone on 0333 323 1982 (option 1) or by emailing hospitality@avfc.co.uk. Under-3s Policy Many supporters may not be aware of the policy regarding under-3s, so we are reminding supporters that under-3’s are only permitted to be in the Family areas in the Trinity Road stand at Villa Park and must have a match ticket. Further information can be found here Aston Villa Ticketing (avfc.co.uk) Visiting Villa Park It's important that supporters take steps to plan their visit to the stadium. Turnstiles open at 1.30pm, so make sure you arrive at the stadium in plenty of time ahead of kick-off to ensure smooth entry. Please note, access to stiles is maintained during the first half and will close once the half-time whistle is blown. However you're planning on getting to the match, take a look at our Travel & Parking guide before leaving home. A traffic exclusion zone will be in operation around the stadium, meaning there will be less traffic on Trinity Road and Witton Lane from up to two hours before the match starts. The Holte Pub will open three hours prior to kick-off and close two hours after full-time. Parking and Travel Aston Villa supporters can pre-book official matchday parking for the first time thanks to new partnerships. Supporters can pre-book their space via either of the links below. www.astonvillaparking.co.uk www.countrywidemanagementsolutions.co.uk Supporters are also advised of the following matchday parking sites around Villa Park: Powerleague (£10 – payment by cash or card) Aston Tower School (£7 - cash only) Birchfield School (£7 - cash only) Covered cycle parking is available at our Inner City Academy on Witton Road. Please plan ahead and leave more time for your journey. To plan your journey by public transport, visit www.tfwm.org.uk. City Centre and Tamworth Shuttle Buses Aston Villa is delighted to confirm to supporters that our AV82 city centre shuttle bus service will continue to run on matchdays this season. This follows a successful trial of the service at the back end of last season. The service will be in operation for all men’s home matches in the Premier League, UEFA Champions League and all domestic cup competitions, helping supporters travel more easily to and from Villa Park. Run by Stagecoach, the AV82 shuttle picks supporters up from outside Birmingham New Street Station on Dudley Street, running directly to Villa Park. Buses run every 15 minutes, starting three hours before kick-off for every home fixture. Services run back to Birmingham New Street at regular intervals for up to 90 minutes after the final whistle of every game, with services departing from Grosvenor Road, on the walking route towards Aston Station. The club also has a dedicated bus service, AV1, that runs to Villa Park from Tamworth on matchday. You can purchase tickets here. More information is available here. Accessibility signage installed at Witton Station Accessibility signage was fitted at Witton Station last season thanks in large part to an Aston Villa supporter. Cameron Wood is a wheelchair user and disability rights campaigner who travels to and from games at Villa Park via train. Cameron wrote to the club to voice his concerns about accessibility at the station on a matchday and a meeting was subsequently arranged with West Midlands Trains. Following the meeting, new accessibility signage, which can be seen from both ends of the platform by station and onboard staff, was fitted. Find out more here. Train Travel There are no planned rail service disruptions this weekend and services to Witton and Aston stations are expected to be running run as normal. Supporters are still encouraged to check your entire journey before you travel. You can check your journey using the National Rail Enquiries real-time journey planner here. If you travel by tram to get to and from Villa Park, please visit the Transport for West Midlands’ journey planner to help plan your journey. Pre-match activities Click on the sections below to learn more about activities taking place at Villa Park before kick-off. New Fan Zone Opens Our new Fan Zone is again open on Saturday, located in the former Players' Car Park. The new space opens its doorts at 12noon, with supporters able to enjoy a bar area, food and big screen TVs. The Fan Zone is also in prime position to watch player arrivals from 1.30pm. Family Fun Zone Unfortunately, due to the planned works, the Academy building is not operational to the extent to which we can safely open the Family Fun Zone. The club are working on alternate arrangements to enable a continuation of some of the family-friendly pre-match activities. Updates will follow in the coming weeks. Food and Beverage Villa Park offers a wide range of cuisine and beverages on a matchday. Outside the stadium, several street food trucks will sit alongside a bar serving alcoholic and soft drinks from 12noon. Cashless Stadium Please note, Villa Park will be cashless to help ensure safe social distancing practices and minimise contact at all points of sale. This includes the Villa Store, with card payments widely accepted in and around the stadium. The only exception is programme kiosks outside the ground, which will still accept cash. In-stadia betting kiosks will also be in operation for this fixture and such service will be provided on a cashless basis. The Warehouse Design Update The Warehouse, which will now be 40,000 sqft, has been further developed with an enhanced design to accommodate live music and entertainment events for 3,500+ visitors and fans. Structural alterations to over half the rooftop will make way for a newly raised flat roof which will accommodate a generous mezzanine floor and viewing galley from above for both live music and fan zone entertainment. Creating a multi-use space called ‘The Warehouse’, will include a diverse range of Food & Beverage offers and pre- and post-match and event entertainment. The Warehouse will become a vibrant and ‘go to’ destination for the community embedded in the Villa Park and Witton Street scene. Anniversary Album and Programme Bundle Fans can pick up a copy of the official 150th Anniversary Sticker Album alongside the matchday programme at Saturday's game. Both publications will be available to buy from sellers around the ground, as well as in the Villa Store, priced at £6 (RRP £8) for the bundle. You can also purchase either separately for £4, with sellers accepting cash or contactless payments. Stickers for the limited edition album must be collected via the programme, the News & Record, which will carry stickers in each of our 19 home Premier League programmes (please note, no stickers are included in cup or Champions League programmes). If you can't get to Villa Park on matchday, copies of both can be purchased online via the link below. Find out more here. Villa Store Click on the sections below to learn about merchandise points around the stadium. Store Opening Hours There are a number of places where supporters can shop for Aston Villa merchandise on a matchday, including the upgraded Villa Store. Below you'll find the opening times for each shop around the ground... Villa Store: 9am - 5.30pm Holte Store: 12-3pm and 4.30-5.30pm 82 Lounge: 12-3pm and 3.30-5.30pm Lower Grounds: 12-3pm and 3.30-5.30pm Trinity: 1.30-3pm and open at half-time During the game Click on the sections below to learn more about events that take place during the match. See it...hear it...report it! If you witness discriminatory behaviour at a match or around a stadium, please report it to the police or a steward. Supporters at Villa Park can text REPORT to 60060 along with the location of the incident and details of what you have witnessed. The text line also includes the reporting of any safeguarding concerns. The text service will be supported by our security team and West Midlands Police. Each text costs one standard message at your network rate. The club may share your details with West Midlands Police if further information is required to assist with a criminal prosecution. All-seater Stadium We would like to remind supporters that Villa Park is an all-seater stadium. In all areas of Villa Park, we have children, elderly fans and those with medical conditions who are unable to see or watch the match if supporters stand up in front of them. Please be mindful of this at all times and thank you for your cooperation. Fan Engagement Click the sections below to learn about how you can get involved on matchday and our fan loyalty programme, Villa Rewards. Get Involved New for 2024, the ‘Villa Predictor’ game will give fans the opportunity to win a £500 store voucher if they correctly predict the outcome of the game. T&Cs apply. We want to know about your matchday experience at Villa Park. Tweet us using #UTV and you could feature on the big screens at the stadium! Pride Rewards The Pride Rewards collection point will be open from 12.30-2.30pm. The Pride Rewards Portal is back up and running. Log in here.

arts.ttl: Unai Emery addresses Ollie Watkins and Jhon Duran debate as he makes starting admission
arts.sum: After bagging three from off the bench in the club's first four league games, his minutes-per-goal average is the best of any Villa player to score four or more times for the club in the Premier League. Progressively he got better and, of course, to compete with Watkins is difficult but he had moments to play and his impact last year was sometimes like now he is doing.

arts.ttl: Team News: Aston Villa vs. Wolverhampton Wanderers injury, suspension list, predicted XIs
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest injury and suspension news ahead of Saturday's Premier League clash between Aston Villa and Wolverhampton Wanderers.

arts.ttl: Unai Emery responds to Ollie Watkins injury worries as Aston Villa absence explained
arts.sum: Ollie Watkins is "100 per cent available" to play against Wolves on Saturday, despite having his ankle iced on the substitutes bench on Tuesday night, but Unai Emery has confirmed Jaden Philogene remains sidelined. Villa will go in search of their fourth Premier League win of the season on Saturday against Wolves, who are winless at the start of the campaign.
